original_abstract: While few individuals have the opportunity to immortalize their life stories, the biblical narrative of Abraham serves as a profound legacy for contemporary believers. Central to this legacy is Abraham’s intercession for Sodom and Gomorrah in Genesis 18:16–33, a compelling account of divine-human negotiation. Despite God’s decree to destroy the cities for their wickedness, Abraham petitioned for mercy, ultimately resulting in the rescue of Lot and his daughters. This study examines Abraham's intercessory role through the lens of Pentecostal spirituality. Employing narrative interpretation and a comprehensive literature review, the research explores the nuances of Abraham’s life and the theological weight of his mediation. The findings suggest that from a Pentecostal perspective, Abraham’s intercession exemplifies a synthesis of persistent faith and compassionate action. Furthermore, this narrative serves as a vital paradigm for the mission of the contemporary church, highlighting a vocation of advocacy empowered by the Holy Spirit.
humanized_abstract: The lives of most people who have ever lived will never be remembered or mentioned in any way. Fortunately for those of us who find ourselves as followers of Abraham, God has given us a remarkable legacy of the man of God through the biblical narrative of Abraham. The most well-known part of this narrative will be the account of Abraham's intercession for Sodom and Gomorrah in Genesis 18:16–33. In this passage God determines to destroy the city because of the wickedness of its people. Abraham, however, acts as an intercessor in a significant way, and as a result Lot and his two daughters are saved. This thesis will investigate Abraham's role as an intercessor through a Pentecostal theology of the narrative of his life and the theological significance of Abraham's intercession. It makes use of narrative interpretation methods, incorporating a full review of the literature associated with Genesis and the life and legacy of Abraham. In doing so, this research reveals that Abraham's intercession is a prime exemplar of the type of faith that is valued in the Pentecostal tradition, which also understands the act of intercession to be an expression of compassion. The intercession of Abraham provides a comprehensive model of what God may be calling the contemporary church to be involved in as a mission in the Spirit.
